reasoning: The only known event that comes close to the $100M threshold is the historic $1.5 billion settlement in Bartz v. Anthropic, which was reached in late August 2025 and granted preliminary approval in September 2025. However, the final approval (fairness) hearing is not scheduled until April or May 2026, with opting out and claims deadlines in January and March 2026, respectively. The criteria require a court to 'order a company to pay >=$100M to claimants' by December 31, 2025. Preliminary approval explicitly does not order a payout to claimants; it only authorizes class notice. Since the final approval will not occur until well into 2026, this event will not trigger a YES before the end of 2025. The community estimate of 1% accurately reflects that no other major impending settlements or fines existed, and the Anthropic settlement timeline decisively falls outside the 2025 window. Thus, the probability of a YES resolution in the final 10 days of the year is exceptionally low.
